page-find-wallet-desc-2: Don’t know what a wallet is?
page-find-wallet-description: Wallets store and transact your ETH. You can choose from a variety of products that tailor to your needs.
page-find-wallet-meta-description: Find and compare Ethereum wallets based on the features you want.
page-find-wallet-try-removing: Try removing a feature or two
page-find-wallet-open-source-desc: Open-source software lets anyone audit the integrity and security of the application
page-find-wallet-non-custodial-desc: Wallets that do not control your private keys
page-find-wallet-hardware-wallet-support: Wallets that can connect to hardware wallet for better security
page-find-wallet-rpc-importing-desc: Wallets supporting custom RPC endpoints to connect to different nodes or networks
page-find-wallet-nft-support-desc: Wallets that support viewing and interacting with your NFTs
page-find-wallet-connect-to-dapps-desc: You can connect to applications that support WalletConnect or an alternative
page-find-wallet-staking-desc: Stake ETH directly from the wallet
page-find-wallet-swaps-desc: Swap ERC-20 tokens directly in the wallet
page-find-wallet-gas-fee-customization-d: Customize your gas amounts (base fee, priority fee, and max fee)
page-find-wallet-ens-support-desc: Wallets that support Ethereum Name Service (ENS)
page-find-wallet-token-importing-desc: Import any ERC-20 token to use in the wallet
page-find-wallet-buy-crypto-desc:
Buy crypto with fiat directly in the wallet 
 *Note: buying crypto may be region specific
page-find-wallet-sell-for-fiat-desc:
Sell crypto to fiat directly in the wallet 
 *Note: withdrawing crypto may be region specific
page-find-wallet-multisig-desc: Wallets that require multiple signatures to authorize a transaction
page-find-wallet-social-recovery-desc: Wallets that allow guardians to change the signing key for smart contract wallets
page-find-wallet-new-to-crypto-desc: First time user looking for beginner wallet.
page-find-wallet-nfts-desc: Wallets with focus on NFT support.
page-find-wallet-hodler-desc: Passive token holding with hardware wallets.
page-find-wallet-finance-desc: Wallets focusing on frequent usage of DeFi apps.
page-find-wallet-developer-desc: Wallets that help develop and test dapps.
page-find-wallet-footnote-1: Wallets listed on this page are not official endorsements, and are provided for informational purposes only.
page-find-wallet-footnote-2: Their descriptions have been provided by the wallet projects themselves.
page-find-wallet-footnote-3: We add products to this page based on criteria in our listing policy. If you'd like us to add a wallet, raise an issue in GitHub.
page-find-wallet-empty-results-desc: There are no wallets matching your criteria, try removing some filters.
page-find-wallet-privacy-desc: Wallets that support built-in private transactions
page-find-wallet-methodology-intro: Every wallet on this page is reviewed by the ethereum.org team before being listed. We apply a published set of criteria focused on security, self-custody, and Ethereum-native support so users can navigate the ecosystem with greater confidence.
page-find-wallet-methodology-must-haves-: To be listed, a wallet must meet the following requirements:
page-find-wallet-methodology-criterion-s: Security-tested through audit, an internal security team, or open-source code review.
page-find-wallet-methodology-criterion-t: Been live for at least six months, or built by a team with an established track record.
page-find-wallet-methodology-criterion-m: Actively maintained, with support available for users.
page-find-wallet-methodology-criterion-h: Provides honest, accurate listing information. Products that falsify details are removed.
page-find-wallet-methodology-criterion-c: Has a named point of contact so we can verify information when it changes.
page-find-wallet-methodology-criterion-e: Supports EIP-1559 (type 2) transactions on Ethereum Mainnet.
page-find-wallet-methodology-criterion-u: Offers a reviewable user experience. If our team finds a product difficult to use, we may request improvements before listing it.
page-find-wallet-methodology-criterion-e: Is Ethereum-focused, with Ethereum or a Layer 2 set as the default network.
page-find-wallet-methodology-verificatio: Listings are not static. Wallet providers are required to resubmit information every six months. If a team does not respond, we remove the wallet. This keeps the directory accurate as products evolve.
page-find-wallet-methodology-filters: Filter toggles on this page (open source, self-custody, hardware wallet support, and others) reflect attributes tracked per wallet. Each listing also shows the date its information was last verified.
